question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: router.getExt(getPath('allowances/crypto'), CryptoAllowanceController.getAccountCryptoAllowances)] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/crypto with a valid EVM address that requires a DB lookup, followed by a crypto allowance query, causing two sequential DB operations per request and under concurrent load exhausting the connection pool and causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: AccountController.extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with timestamp=gte:0, causing extractStakingRewardsQuery() to generate a query with a lower bound of 0 that scans all staking reward transfers for the account, holding a DB connection open for the full scan duration and contributing to a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TokenAllowanceController.getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/tokens with a valid alias that requires a DB lookup, followed by a three-part UNION token allowance query, causing two sequential DB operations per request and under concurrent load exhausting the connection pool and causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: BaseController.getUpperFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with token.id=lte:X&serialnumber=lte:Y, causing getUpperFilters() to generate an upper UNION query that scans all NFTs up to the specified bounds, holding a DB connection open for the full scan duration and contributing to a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: router.getExt(getPath('tokens'), TokenController.getTokenRelationships)] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/tokens with a valid EVM address that requires a DB lookup, followed by an isValidAccount() check and a token relationship query, causing three sequential DB operations per request and under concurrent load exhausting the connection pool and causing a network partition?
